page.content: In the evolving landscape of European e-commerce, the advertising revenue generated from marketplaces has shown significant growth, with projections indicating an increase to CHF1.5B in 2024, reflecting a robust 29% year-on-year growth.

lines.text: In the evolving landscape of European e-commerce, the advertising revenue generated from marketplaces has shown significant
growth, with projections indicating an increase to CHF1.5B in 2024, reflecting a robust 29% year-on-year growth.

page.content: This trend underscores the critical importance of optimized payment methods, as illustrated by the preference for CreditCard transactions, which accounted for 45% of the total in 2023. Additionally, the effectiveness of live commerce initiatives is evidenced by a conversion rate of 5.2%, which, while promising, highlights the need for ongoing improvements in customer engagement strategies. Notably, in 2023, the marketplace's revenue was CHF1.2B, with an impressive 84% attributed to targeted advertising efforts. Furthermore, payment method preferences reveal that BankTransfer's conversion rate was 3.5%, while PayPal transactions represented 30% of the total, with a 5.2% conversion rate, indicating varying levels of effectiveness among payment options.

page.content: The data also points to the necessity for continuous refinement, as seen in the 82% conversion rate for live commerce initiatives that generated CHF250M in revenue from 15M transactions.

page.content: As we move forward, these insights will be pivotal for stakeholders aiming to harness the full potential of the e-commerce market.
